findings.line: The stakes pointed outward. The cavalry lanes left room for a charge. Healers sat near the middle, where men could be hauled in from an open fight. Bread wagons stood close to the kitchens, tarped against rain. Steam ghosted up beyond them.
findings.reason: Five consecutive sentences follow the same Subject-Verb-Object template (The [noun] [past-tense verb]). Rhythmic monotone in camp inventory.
findings.suggestedFix: Stakes outward. Cavalry lanes wide enough for a charge — healers near the middle, close enough to haul men back from an open fight. Bread wagons tarped by the kitchens. Steam beyond them, thin and sour.

findings.line: His brows drew together.
findings.reason: Generic brow-furrow beat. Could describe any character in any scene. Falric has specific physicality (door-shouldered guard) but this beat is stock.
findings.suggestedFix: Something moved behind Falric's eyes — the reflex of a man deciding whether to escalate.

findings.line: Uther went still.
findings.reason: Generic stillness beat. Three-word stock reaction. Uther has been physically established (big hands, thickened by age) but this beat is interchangeable.
findings.suggestedFix: Uther's hands lifted from the table edge and hung at his sides, empty.

findings.line: Enough of it showed in Falric's face that she knew rumor had beaten the formal brief.
findings.reason: Filter verb 'she knew' is scaffolding. The observation stands without it. We are already in Sylvanas's POV.
findings.suggestedFix: Enough of it showed in Falric's face — rumor had beaten the formal brief.

findings.line: the absence no longer carried irritation in Sylvanas's head
findings.reason: Redundant POV qualifier 'in Sylvanas's head'. Every unattributed thought is already in her head. The phrase is scaffolding.

findings.line: A kingdom drove itself toward the answer it wanted least.
findings.reason: Closure-addiction aphorism. Thematic epigram capping a paragraph. Stronger ending at 'Here the wet ground swallowed half the noise.'
findings.fixNote: DELETE — paragraph ends stronger at the preceding sentence.

findings.line: Catastrophe now had cavalry.
findings.reason: Closure-addiction epigram. One-line thematic summary between action beats. Scene reads cleaner without it.
findings.fixNote: DELETE — connect watching to Cyndia's arrival.

findings.line: because they had split the work sensibly and been paying for it in blood ever since
findings.reason: Retrospective thematic clause. Sentence is stronger ending at 'an island keep Jaina had entered alone.'
findings.suggestedFix: End sentence at 'an island keep Jaina had entered alone.'

findings.line: South, the fuse burned. North, the hand that lit it reached for the only thing in this country she still trusted not to break.
findings.reason: Closure-addiction double epigram as chapter coda. Chapter already ends at 'Sylvanas bent low over the mare's neck and drove harder' — an action close. These lines repackage meaning in thematic summary.
findings.fixNote: DELETE both lines. Chapter ends at line 273.

clusterWarnings: lines 105-106: 2 room-agency kills in same paragraph

findings.line: the land still gave itself away
findings.subject: land
findings.realAgent: field damage, crop patterns, visible signs of plague
findings.reason: Scenery given self-revealing intent. The land doesn't give itself away — Sylvanas reads the signs.
findings.suggestedFix: and every fence line showed it

findings.line: The room bent toward Arthas's urgency.
findings.subject: room
findings.realAgent: officers, advisors — the people in the room
findings.reason: Infrastructure given emotional response. The room doesn't bend — the people orient toward Arthas.
findings.suggestedFix: Every officer at the table leaned toward Arthas.

findings.line: It steadied around Uther's silence.
findings.subject: it (the room)
findings.realAgent: officers — they stopped moving, deferred to Uther
findings.reason: Room given stabilizing response to silence. Two-line room-agency cluster with preceding sentence. P6/P7.
findings.suggestedFix: Uther said nothing, and nobody moved to fill it.

findings.line: Each answer sharpened him and took one more layer of caution out of the room.
findings.subject: each answer / caution
findings.realAgent: Arthas's growing confidence, officers following his lead
findings.reason: Abstract object (answer) modifying a person and environment. Answers don't sharpen people or remove caution from rooms.
findings.suggestedFix: With each answer he sharpened, and the officers stopped qualifying their questions.

findings.line: A kingdom drove itself toward the answer it wanted least.
findings.subject: kingdom
findings.reason: Institution given self-direction and desire. Also caught by F7 — double trigger.

findings.line: North waited under mist, stone, and an island keep
findings.subject: North
findings.reason: Geography given human patience. Repeated 'waited' with line 271.
findings.suggestedFix: North lay under mist, stone, and an island keep Jaina had entered alone.

findings.line: Caer Darrow waited somewhere beyond
findings.subject: Caer Darrow
findings.realAgent: the fortress exists in a location
findings.reason: Location given human patience. Repeats 'waited' from line 253.

findings.line: Quartermaster scribes bent over manifests, still believing ink could outrun catastrophe.
findings.reason: KEEP — 'believing' is attributed to the scribes (human agents). Sylvanas's contemptuous read of bureaucratic behavior. Character-voice defense.

findings.line: the number of ways the whole place assumed a visible war
findings.reason: KEEP — Sylvanas counting tactical postures. The camp's organization IS oriented around visible war — her assessment of defensive orientation. Subject-matter defense.

findings.line: Cyndia mounted silence the way other people wore wool
findings.reason: KEEP — human character IS the agent. Cyndia is choosing silence. Correct attribution.

findings.line: the camp had already turned itself inside out
findings.reason: KEEP — near-literal description of a military camp breaking down for march. The physical process of mobilization.

findings.line: weariness took the place where the grief had been
findings.reason: KEEP — emotional succession metaphor inside Sylvanas's read of Uther's face. POV emotional read, not infrastructure agency.

findings.line: Human ink chasing a thing already inside their bread.
findings.reason: KEEP — Sylvanas's contemptuous metaphor for bureaucratic futility. Character-voice defense.

findings.line: Evening gathered under the trees.
findings.reason: KEEP — near-literal description of dusk collecting in shaded terrain. Visibility change a ranger would note.

povRegister.summary: Tactical military commander, ~1500-year-old elven aristocrat. Reads rooms for exits, threats, competence. Thinks in compressed finished judgments and military shorthand. Notices physical details: rank insignia, weapon quality, body contradictions, terrain. Contemptuous of bureaucracy and emotional process. Would NOT think in philosophical abstractions, thematic summaries, or literary metaphors outside military/political domain.

findings.line: That landed.
findings.whoIsSaying: Narrator — generic recognition marker that could appear in any POV
findings.povMismatch: Sylvanas reads faces for specific signals. 'That landed' is a generic verdict-tag; her version would be a concrete facial observation.
findings.reason: Verdict-tag fragment. Generic scaffolding before the useful observation. Deletion improves: the passage reads 'Not understanding. Weight. Enough of it showed in Falric's face...'
findings.fixNote: DELETE — 'Not understanding. Weight.' anchors directly to Falric's face.

findings.line: There it was.
findings.povMismatch: 'There it was' is a narrative staging cue, not a Sylvanas-specific thought. Her recognition would be concrete: what she sees in Arthas.
findings.reason: Verdict-tag fragment. 'The speed of him.' follows and is stronger without scaffolding.
findings.fixNote: DELETE — 'The speed of him.' carries the observation alone.

findings.line: He had spent half his life dragging boys back from the kinds of certainty they mistook for courage.
findings.whoIsSaying: Author speaking through Sylvanas — the aphoristic quality exceeds the character's in-scene register
findings.povMismatch: 'The kinds of certainty they mistook for courage' is essayistic abstraction. Sylvanas would note Uther's weariness in tactical terms: he's been pulling young commanders back from lethal conviction his whole career. The polished phrasing reads written, not thought.
findings.reason: Sentence too polished for POV. The aphorism 'certainty they mistook for courage' is author-grade compression. Sylvanas would think in more direct military terms.
findings.suggestedFix: He had spent half his life pulling boys back from the calls that would cost them everything.

findings.line: because love and duty had both failed him and he had no third thing left
findings.whoIsSaying: Narrator commenting on Uther's tragedy — philosophical categories exceed Sylvanas's tactical register
findings.povMismatch: 'Love and duty' as abstract category failures with 'no third thing left' as numbered-list exhaustion is philosophical framing. Sylvanas would think: he can't save Arthas and can't walk away.
findings.reason: Conceptual compression beyond POV register. 'Love and duty had both failed him' frames Uther's predicament in philosophical terms; 'no third thing left' is literary abstraction.
findings.suggestedFix: because he could not save Arthas from himself and could not walk away

findings.line: So did every choice already made.
findings.whoIsSaying: Narrator generalizing after two concrete references
findings.povMismatch: After 'The ghost had a place in the line. So did the missing mage.' (concrete, specific), 'every choice already made' escalates into thematic summary. Sylvanas would remain concrete.
findings.reason: Thematic awareness. Abstract generalization after concrete specifics. The preceding two items (ghost, missing mage) are strong; the third pivots to narrator-level commentary.
findings.fixNote: DELETE — the line reads stronger ending at 'So did the missing mage.'

findings.line: Worked. Sealed. Built to keep.
findings.reason: KEEP — fragments add concrete factual information about granary state (reinforced, airtight, purpose-built). Active military infrastructure assessment.

findings.line: Supply traffic, once.
findings.reason: KEEP — temporal tactical assessment of old tracks. 'Once' is the diagnostic finding: this was routine commerce, now it's stopped.

findings.line: That was the count now.
findings.reason: KEEP — active force assessment. 'Now' marks temporal degradation. Sylvanas is doing casualty accounting as part of operational planning.

findings.line: Proper force, gathered in a hurry.
findings.reason: KEEP — tactical assessment of the camp she's about to enter. Affects her approach: real army, hastily assembled.

findings.line: That bought one heartbeat.
findings.reason: KEEP — Sylvanas measuring verbal maneuver effect in heartbeats. Active tactical timing in character.

findings.line: Not wiser. Harder.
findings.reason: KEEP — character-voice defense. Finished judgment IS Sylvanas's cognitive mode per voice sheet. Binary diagnostic of Arthas's change that affects her briefing approach.

findings.line: That was a lie, and the speed with which he needed it chilled her more than the order.
findings.reason: KEEP — deception detection (tactical read) + emotional response (chilled). Both inside Sylvanas's perspective and decision-making.

findings.line: The border fiction was dead in ink if nowhere else.
findings.reason: KEEP — political/tactical assessment. 'Border fiction' is Sylvanas's term for the Convocation pretense. 'Dead in ink' is her read of Lor'themar's dispatch.

findings.line: So did the last chance to pretend the plague could be solved by telling the right men and letting rank take it from there.
findings.reason: KEEP — tactical realization about closed options, not narrative commentary. She's recognizing that institutional delegation has failed and she's committed to direct action.

findings.line: Grief held under discipline.
findings.reason: KEEP — military face-reading. Identifies the emotion (grief) and the control mechanism (discipline). Exactly how a military commander categorizes what she sees.

findings.line: He was an old campaign scar given shoulders.
findings.reason: KEEP — military metaphor from Sylvanas's domain. Campaign scars and shoulders are within her experiential world.

audits.keepRateNote: KEEP rate above 20% threshold. Justified: POV character is a military intelligence analyst. Diagnostic absence (what ISN'T there) is her primary analytical mode — reading tracks for missing panic, faces for missing comprehension, dispatches for missing speculation. The binary-diagnostic keeps are genuine in-scene assessments that change her tactical decisions.

findings.line: the absence no longer carried irritation in Sylvanas's head. It carried cost.
findings.deletionTest: 'the absence carried cost' — survives. Clean positive statement delivers the key information.
findings.reason: Source-swap: refuses the prior state (irritation) before naming the current (cost). Positive rewrite is cleaner. Co-triggered with F3 on same line.
findings.suggestedFix: Jaina was not here, and the absence carried cost.

findings.line: No pause spent on whether belief ought to hurt.
findings.deletionTest: 'He kept asking questions, good ones, and too few.' — survives and improves. The philosophical abstraction is removed.
findings.reason: Preamble negation scaffolding. Also exceeds Sylvanas's register ('whether belief ought to hurt' is philosophical). Co-triggered with narrator-seep B2.
findings.fixNote: DELETE — combined with A1 deletion of 'There it was.' the passage becomes: 'The speed of him. He kept asking questions, good ones, and too few.'

findings.line: Not a question.
findings.deletionTest: 'You know what he is,' he said. — survives. Reader already knows it's a statement from the phrasing.
findings.reason: Minimizer: bats away one interpretation (question) that the reader never held. Scaffolding.
findings.fixNote: DELETE — Uther's line reads as a statement without the label.

findings.line: No panic in the spacing. No snapped leather.
findings.deletionTest: Loses diagnostic information about track condition. Does not survive.
findings.reason: Binary diagnostic (condition 1). 'No panic' distinguishes calm commerce from flight — both real possibilities in plague country. Changes Sylvanas's tactical read of the terrain.

findings.line: Not understanding. Weight.
findings.deletionTest: 'Weight' alone lacks context. Does not survive.
findings.reason: Binary diagnostic (condition 1). Sylvanas distinguishes between two possible reactions on Falric's face: comprehension vs. emotional impact. The distinction affects her next move.

findings.line: Not wiser. Harder.
findings.deletionTest: 'Harder' after 'Command had cut it harder' creates odd repetition. Does not survive.
findings.reason: Binary diagnostic (condition 1). Wiser vs. harder: distinguishes improvement from intensification. Changes how she approaches the briefing.

findings.line: Not all of it. Not Jaina inside Caer Darrow. Not Kel'Thuzad's full shape under the work.
findings.deletionTest: Loses critical plot information: WHAT she withheld. Does not survive.
findings.reason: Hard constraint (condition 3). Three real operational constraints on information sharing that affect the parallel plot thread. She CAN'T reveal these without compromising Jaina's mission.

findings.line: No detail. No guessing. Good.
findings.deletionTest: 'Good' alone tells reader nothing about dispatch quality. Does not survive.
findings.reason: Binary diagnostic (condition 1). Speculative vs. factual intelligence. The absence of speculation is the quality marker that tells Sylvanas she can trust this dispatch.

instances: Line 215: 'The corner of his mouth moved once.' (Uther)
findings.reason: At cap (1/1). Single instance is the strongest use — Uther's almost-smile after Sylvanas's 'It won't.' Character-specific and earned.
Zero instances of 'marked' as a verb. 'Marker' (line 15, saint's marker) and 'marks' (line 229, relay marks) are physical nouns, not the tic pattern.

findings.note: Line 5 'tighten the air' is metaphorical/environmental, not a body part. Not counted against cap. Line 159 'jaw set' uses 'set' not 'tightened'.
findings.reason: Well within cap (1/3 tightened, 0/2 eased). Single instance is emotional but character-specific to Cyndia.
Zero instances of 'shifted' for facial expressions.

selfCheck.physicalVsEmotional: 1 emotional tightened (Cyndia), 0 physical. Within cap, no kill needed.
selfCheck.slophunterIntroducedTics: Cannot determine without v1 comparison. No kills needed regardless.

summary.verdictReason: 24 KILL verdicts across 4 guides (gpt-5: 9, intent-smear: 7, narrator-seep: 5, negation: 3). 24 with suggested fixes, 0 unfixable. Top patterns: F7 Closure Addiction (4), P6 Scenery Revealing (4), A1 Verdict-Tag (2). Cross-trigger on line 183 (F7+P4). Recurring tics clean.
